_meta.purpose: Stage 8.4.2 — Wolfhouse Somo pricing/payment fixture for the quote calculator (8.4.3+). This file is the canonical pricing source for manual booking creation. All monetary values are in CENTS. Load this file; do not hardcode prices in application code.
notes: Package prices are CONFIRMED by owner (Ale, Stage 3x.2f/2g).
Add-on prices are CONFIRMED by Ale (Stage 3x.2g).
Fields marked REQUIRED_FROM_STAFF must not be used for autonomous pricing.
Fields marked CONFIRMED may be used for automatic quotes and payment links.
PRORATION_FORMULA_AMBIGUITY: two interpretations exist (see rounding section).

_seasons_note: Season is determined by the check-in month number. August (month 8) has priority 10 and overrides the summer rule. Months 3 (March) and 11 (November) exist in the baseline as shoulder but have no seeded package_price_rules row — see missing_required_values.

seasons._note: August overrides summer when check-in month is 8 (matches package_price_rules.priority=10)
December, January, February closed. Do not quote or book.

_packages_note: Prices are PER PERSON PER WEEK in a SHARED room, in CENTS. Select the seasonal price by matching the check-in month to the season with the highest priority. Lookup order: find all seasons containing the check-in month, take the one with the highest priority value.

room_supplements._note: Applied on top of the package base price. shared = no supplement (modifier = 0).

double._note: +EUR10 per person per night on top of the shared base (Stage 3x.2f, confirmed by owner)

private._note: Same supplement as double. Confirm if private-room pricing differs from double.

rounding._note: PRORATION_FORMULA_AMBIGUITY: two interpretations exist in the source docs. Both give the same result for 4-night Uluwatu but differ for 3-night Malibu (see _examples). The quote calculator (8.4.3) must resolve this before going live.
rounding._formula_A_whole_stay: per_person_cents = CEIL5(weekly_per_person_eur * nights / 7) * 100; then * guest_count. Source: package-pricing.md (shows 110 for 3n Malibu spring).
rounding._formula_B_per_night: per_night_eur = CEIL5(weekly_per_person_eur / 7); per_person_cents = per_night_eur * nights * 100; then * guest_count. Source: wolfhouse-somo.baseline.json 3x.2g example.

3n_malibu_spring_1_guest.formula_B: CEIL5(249/7)=CEIL5(35.57)=40 EUR/night; 40*3=120 EUR per person -> 12000 cents

4n_uluwatu_spring_1_guest.formula_B: CEIL5(349/7)=CEIL5(49.86)=50 EUR/night; 50*4=200 EUR per person -> 20000 cents

7n_malibu_spring_1_guest.formula_B: CEIL5(249/7)=40 EUR/night; 40*7=280 EUR per person -> 28000 cents
7n_malibu_spring_1_guest._note: Formula A returns 250 (near 249); formula B returns 280. For the standard 7-night stay, use the flat weekly price directly (249 EUR = 24900 cents) -- no proration needed.

rounding.seven_night_rule: For stays of exactly 7 nights, use the flat weekly_per_person_cents directly — no proration needed.
rounding._formula_resolve_note: Confirm with Ale which formula applies. Package-pricing.md example shows Formula A. Baseline 3x.2g description implies Formula B but its example (4n Uluwatu) is ambiguous.

deposits._note: Deposit tier is confirmed by Ale (Stage 3x.2g). scope=per_booking is confirmed.

standard_package._match: Named package (malibu, uluwatu, or waimea) at standard terms

custom_or_short_stay._match: Custom-built package OR non-7-night / shorter stay

deposits._default_note: Fallback when booking type is ambiguous; resolve type before quoting deposit, else handoff.
deposits._required_note: Confirm: does the Waimea or Uluwatu deposit differ from Malibu? Does double/private room change the deposit?

add_ons._note: All prices confirmed by Ale (Stage 3x.2g). charge_timing is REQUIRED_FROM_STAFF for rentals.

wetsuit_rental.name: Wetsuit rental
wetsuit_rental._charge_timing_note: Confirm: charged with booking deposit/payment link, or tracked and paid on site?

wetsuit_soft_top_combo.name: Wetsuit + Soft board combo
wetsuit_soft_top_combo._note: Promo: same price as soft board alone — wetsuit free. Combos and individual rentals bill independently when both selected (26j.2).

wetsuit_hard_board_combo.name: Wetsuit + hard board combo
wetsuit_hard_board_combo._note: Promo: same price as hard board alone — wetsuit free. Combos and individual rentals bill independently when both selected (26j.2).

surf_lesson_multi.name: Surf lesson (2+ bundle rate)
surf_lesson_multi._note: When booking 2 or more lessons, ALL lessons (including the first) are priced at EUR30 each.

yoga_class.name: Yoga class
yoga_class._note: Normally booked and paid on site. May be included in special camps — handoff for special-camp requests.

handoff_rules._note: Automatic quote allowed only when all required fields are known and confirmed. Handoff when any of the following apply.

missing_required_values.description: Confirm whether Formula A (ceil5 on whole stay) or Formula B (ceil5 per night then multiply) is the correct proration. They give different results for most stay lengths. Formula A from package-pricing.md shows EUR110 for 3 nights Malibu spring; Formula B gives EUR120.
Confirm: does the deposit amount differ between Malibu (EUR200), Uluwatu, and Waimea packages? Current assumption: EUR200 for all named packages.
Confirm: does booking a double/private room change the deposit amount?
Confirm for each rental (wetsuit, soft-top, hard board): is the add-on charged with the booking deposit/payment link, or tracked and paid on site? Current assumption: on site.
Any group discount tiers, promo codes, or discount mechanics? Current assumption: none — all handoff.
Retreats, special camps, and events have custom pricing. Current assumption: all handoff to staff.
Operator/tour-operator block pricing (often EUR0 / negotiated). Current assumption: always handoff.
For stays longer than 7 nights (e.g. 10, 14 nights): confirm the formula. Is it simply weekly_price * (nights/7) rounded? Is there a discount for multi-week stays?
Months 3 (March) and 11 (November) appear as 'shoulder' in wolfhouse-somo.baseline.json but have NO seeded row in package_price_rules. Are they bookable? If so, do they use the spring_autumn prices?
